personal.name: Mehedi Hasan
personal.title: Every Validator Says Pass. Your Inbox Doesn't Agree.
personal.subtitle: I find the deliverability failures that MXToolbox misses: DKIM breaking on reply paths, SPF hitting RFC lookup limits, DMARC generating zero forensic data. Cold email agencies and B2B outbound teams come to me when every validator says pass and inbox placement still drops. 1,235+ domains recovered.

profile.philosophy: Most deliverability tools check that your records exist. I check whether they work on replies, forwards, and forwarded-to-spam paths. That is where most failures actually happen.
profile.body:
Cold email agencies and B2B outbound teams come to me when inbox placement is declining and every surface-level check says it should not be. The problem is almost never copy or targeting. It is the infrastructure layer: SPF breaking on forwards, DKIM misaligning on replies, DMARC generating zero visibility, warmup and campaign timing creating a detectable fingerprint for ESP filters.

I am an Email Deliverability Consultant. I do not add records and call it done. I run a forensic audit of your sending architecture and identify the specific failure that standard tools cannot see. My clients are outbound-led agencies and SaaS teams running 5 to 50 sending domains, where a two-week deliverability failure translates directly into lost revenue and missed pipeline.
profile.notice: > NOTE: I work exclusively with outbound teams running cold email infrastructure. Single senders, newsletter operators, and transactional systems are outside my scope.

capabilities.title: Multi-domain Sending Pools
capabilities.description: Architecting decentralized sender networks to isolate risk and maximize throughput.

capabilities.title: Deliverability Recovery

capabilities.description: Algorithmic IP and domain cycling to bypass aggressive provider throttling.

capabilities.description: Direct intervention and delisting protocols with major spam monitoring databases.

capabilities.title: Mailbox Provisioning Systems

capabilities.title: Inbox Placement Monitoring

process.title: Discovery & Audit
process.description: A 20-minute discovery call to scope the problem, followed by a forensic audit of your sending architecture, DNS records, and domain reputation data.

process.title: Diagnose & Report
process.description: You receive a written findings report with the specific cause and specific fix. Not a generic checklist of records that passed validation.

process.title: Fix & Implement
process.description: Targeted remediation across your infrastructure: DNS authentication, platform configuration, warmup architecture, and fleet isolation where needed.

process.title: Monitor & Protect
process.description: Ongoing DMARC reporting, reputation monitoring, and inbox placement testing keeps your infrastructure stable, catching issues weeks before reply rates move.

cases.initialFailure: Volume-induced domain burnout. Client attempted to scale from 50k to 250k monthly sends using a single Google Workspace tenant, resulting in global throttling and critical blacklist placement.
cases.rootCause: Lack of domain rotation, identical IP reputation clustering, and missing DMARC quarantine enforcement.
cases.infrastructureActions: Decommissioned burned infrastructure. Provisioned a 20-domain rotating cluster across separate tenants. Implemented strict SPF/DKIM alignment and custom warmup algorithms.
cases.outcome: Restored 94%+ inbox placement and successfully scaled to 300k monthly volume without triggering spam traps.

cases.initialFailure: Operational and transactional emails landing in spam due to outbound marketing activities originating from the root corporate domain.
cases.rootCause: Shared IP/Domain reputation contamination between marketing automation and transactional systems.
cases.infrastructureActions: Architected a subdomain isolation strategy. Migrated all cold outbound to dedicated secondary domains with independent MX records. Hardened root domain DMARC to p=reject.
cases.outcome: 100% isolation achieved. Transactional delivery restored to 99.9% while outbound marketing continued unimpeded on parallel infrastructure.

cta.heading: Every Week You Wait, Your Domain Reputation Gets Harder to Recover.
